summary: The code correctly avoids shell injection by using subprocess.run with a list and shell=False, but the SAST tool (bandit:B603) flags the subprocess call because user-controlled image and cmd values are only partially validated, leaving a risk of Docker argument injection (e.g., image name starting with '--'). The fixer loop attempted to resolve this three times but never addressed the core validation gaps, resulting in a persistent unresolved finding.
suggested_actions: Strengthen image validation to reject strings starting with '-' to prevent Docker argument injection
Enhance cmd validation to block all shell metacharacters (not just ';' and '&') or use a strict allowlist of safe characters
Consider using a dedicated Docker SDK or a more restrictive argument parser to avoid passing untrusted strings directly to docker run
Add explicit comments or a security wrapper around subprocess.run to document the validation measures and justify the remaining risk

evidence.description: subprocess call - check for execution of untrusted input.
